title: Holy Places in the vicinity of Purushottam-Kshetra

analysis.core_story_summary: Dwelling in length upon the majesty of Purushottam kshetra, goddess Laxmi said--Purushottam kshetra resembles a conch in shape and is graced by the presence of lord Shiva. Towards its north is situated a temple of lord Neelkanth. A famous shiva linga named Kapal mochan is also situated in the vicinity.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Contains conflict imagery that may need age-appropriate framing.

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **Holy Places in the vicinity of Purushottam-Kshetra — Kids (8-12):** Dwelling in length upon the majesty of Purushottam kshetra, goddess Laxmi said--Purushottam kshetra resembles a conch in shape and is graced by the presence of lord Shiva. Towards its north is situated a temple of lord Neelkanth. A famous shiva linga named Kapal mochan is also situated in the vicinity. This story teaches devotion and disciplined practice, righteous duty, the cost of conflict. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.
